summary: Align packaged M4 thumbnail evidence with the canonical prefixed geometry-identity contract.

decisions.decision: Require thumbnail renderIdentity values to match exactly sha256:[a-f0-9]{64} in both live walkthrough validation and retained-artifact revalidation; keep raw artifact digests as bare 64-character hex values.
decisions.rationale: The application geometry-identity contract, render cache, viewer, and thumbnail persistence all use the algorithm-prefixed form. Exact clean evidence reached the thumbnail phase and retained a valid prefixed identity, while the M4 harness alone expected the wrong bare shape. Separating geometry identities from raw file digests preserves both contracts and rejects algorithm-confusion lookalikes.

testEvidence.criterion: The M4 walkthrough and retained verifier must accept only the product's exact prefixed SHA-256 geometry identity.
testEvidence.test: Use canonical prefixed identities in both fixtures and explicitly tamper the retained identity to sha512 with the same digest length.

red.outcome: RED: 3 failed, 18 passed. Both validators rejected the product's canonical sha256-prefixed fixture identity.

green.outcome: GREEN: 76/76 tests passed, including rejection of the sha512-prefixed tamper.

nearMisses.sought: Exact clean packaged native M4 thumbnail evidence
nearMisses.confirmation: The run passed volume delta and animation, then failed solely because the harness rejected the retained canonical sha256-prefixed thumbnail identity; cleanup passed.

questions: No owner decision is required; this restores the already-established application geometry-identity contract in release evidence.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
